Q.--Do you think the judiciary
ought to be criticized"


A.--The newspapers ougt to adj
here very carefully to the facts in
discussing or criticizing the decis-
ions of a judge. There is no objec-
ton If they adhere to the strict
facts.


Q.-But suppose objection is
made to the system?


A.-That is to advocate anarchy.
II Is better that we should have
courts, and the courts must be up
held. We have to deal with con-
(fitions as they are. You can't
achieve the ideal There are bound
to be imperfections. 1t is better
to obey the law and submit to the
orders of a court, than to become
lawless. 1t is impossible to have a
perfect system. SOMEBODY WILL
ALWAYS SUFFER- INJUSTICE.

Al can't say that do. But II

Q.--Have any men connected

cess for violations of the Sherman

A.-Not that have heard of. hut

Q.--Were not the Chicago pack

A.-THAT is NO BUSINESS OF
